summary: Rounding to a friendlier nearby number lets you estimate a result quickly and check whether an exact answer is reasonable.

formulas.name: Rounding rule
formulas.expression: \text{round up if the next digit is} \ge 5\text{, otherwise round down}

explanation: To round to a given place value, look at the digit immediately to its right: if it's 5 or more, round up; otherwise, round down. Estimation is most useful as a sanity check — before computing an exact answer, round the numbers involved to get a ballpark figure, then confirm the exact computation lands near that estimate. On the GRE, estimation is also a legitimate shortcut on multiple-choice questions where the answer choices are far apart: if a quick estimate clearly points to one choice, there's no need for exact computation.
